mcqs.question: What is the Subset Sum Problem?

options.text: Finding the maximum subset that sums to W
Determining if any subset equals exactly W
Finding all subsets that sum to W
Finding the minimum number of elements to sum to W

mcqs.explanation: The Subset Sum Problem is a decision problem that asks whether there exists a subset of given integers that adds up exactly to a target sum W. It outputs YES or NO.

mcqs.question: To which complexity class does Subset Sum belong?
mcqs.explanation: Subset Sum is NP-Complete - it is in NP and every problem in NP can be reduced to it in polynomial time. It is one of Karp's 21 NP-complete problems.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of the dynamic programming solution?
mcqs.explanation: The DP solution creates an n×W table where n is number of elements and W is target sum. Each entry takes O(1) time, giving O(n×W) complexity.

mcqs.question: Why is the DP solution called pseudo-polynomial?

options.text: It depends on the value of W not the input size

mcqs.explanation: Pseudo-polynomial means complexity is polynomial in the numeric value of input (W) but exponential in the number of input bits (log W). When W is large, the algorithm becomes inefficient.

mcqs.question: In the DP table dp[i][j], what does it represent?

options.text: Maximum sum using first i elements
Whether subset of first i elements can sum to j
Minimum elements needed to sum to j
Number of ways to sum to j using first i elements

mcqs.explanation: dp[i][j] is a boolean indicating whether some subset of the first i elements (arr[0] to arr[i-1]) can sum exactly to j.

mcqs.question: What is the base case in Subset Sum DP?

options.text: dp[0][0] = true and dp[i][0] = true for all i

mcqs.explanation: dp[0][0] = true (empty set sums to 0) and dp[i][0] = true (any set can form sum 0 with empty subset). dp[0][j>0] = false.

mcqs.question: What is the recurrence for dp[i][j]?

options.text: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] OR dp[i-1][j-arr[i-1]] if j >= arr[i-1]

mcqs.explanation: A subset can sum to j either by not including arr[i-1] (dp[i-1][j]) or by including it and forming j-arr[i-1] with remaining elements (dp[i-1][j-arr[i-1]]).

mcqs.question: What is the Partition Problem a special case of?
mcqs.explanation: Partition Problem asks if a set can be divided into two subsets with equal sum. This is Subset Sum with target = total_sum/2.

mcqs.question: What does FPTAS stand for?
mcqs.explanation: FPTAS (Fully Polynomial Time Approximation Scheme) provides solution within (1±ε) of optimal in time polynomial in both input size and 1/ε.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of backtracking approach?
mcqs.explanation: Backtracking tries all 2^n combinations (each element either included or excluded), giving exponential time complexity.

mcqs.question: How can space complexity of DP be reduced from O(n×W) to O(W)?

options.text: Using 1D array and iterating j backwards

mcqs.explanation: When using 1D array dp[j], iterate j from W down to arr[i] to avoid using the current element twice. This maintains only the previous row's information.

mcqs.question: Which approach divides set into two halves for Subset Sum?
mcqs.explanation: Meet-in-the-Middle splits the set into two halves, computes all subset sums for each half (2^(n/2) each), then uses hashing to find pairs that sum to target.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of Meet-in-the-Middle?
mcqs.explanation: Storing all subset sums of each half requires O(2^(n/2)) space. This is better than O(2^n) but still exponential.

mcqs.question: In which field is Subset Sum NOT directly applicable?
mcqs.explanation: Subset Sum is used in cryptography (knapsack cryptosystems), resource allocation, and financial portfolio optimization. It is not typically used for sorting.

mcqs.question: What type of algorithm is the approximation scheme for Subset Sum?
mcqs.explanation: FPTAS runs in polynomial time in both n and 1/ε, providing approximate solutions when exact solutions are computationally infeasible.

mcqs.question: What happens if the target sum W is 0?
mcqs.explanation: The empty subset always sums to 0. So if target W=0, the answer is always YES regardless of the input set.

mcqs.question: Which sorting step can optimize backtracking?
mcqs.explanation: Sorting in descending order helps pruning in backtracking. When current sum plus remaining sorted elements cannot reach target, we can prune that branch.

mcqs.question: What is the relationship between Subset Sum and 0/1 Knapsack?

options.text: 0/1 Knapsack is a generalization of Subset Sum
Subset Sum is a special case of 0/1 Knapsack (value = weight)

mcqs.explanation: When all values equal weights in 0/1 Knapsack, it becomes Subset Sum. Both are NP-complete and have similar DP solutions.

mcqs.question: What is pruning in backtracking for Subset Sum?

options.text: Skipping branches that cannot lead to solution

mcqs.explanation: Pruning eliminates recursive branches that cannot possibly yield a valid solution: when current sum exceeds target, or when even including all remaining elements cannot reach target.

mcqs.question: If all numbers are positive, when can backtracking prune a branch?

options.text: When sum is less than target

mcqs.explanation: With positive numbers, once the current sum exceeds target, adding more elements will only increase the sum further, so that branch can be safely pruned.
